llm_result.summary: The case presents nighttime cognitive arousal with content-specific replay and anticipatory worry, leading to delayed sleep onset and morning irritability. A set of modifiable levers across biological, psychological, and social/environmental domains (and their interactions) is proposed to reduce nocturnal hyperarousal, reframe pre-sleep cognition, regularize sleep timing, and improve morning mood. Each lever targets one or more of the operationalized criteria (C01–C05) and includes feasible implementation, measurable outcomes, and a clear mechanism of change.

solutions.predictor_variable: Fixed, structured wind-down routine with consistent bedtime and pre-sleep ritual (screen-offs, breathing/relaxation, dim lighting)
solutions.solution_candidate: Implement a 60-minute pre-sleep wind-down: screen-off, 10 minutes of diaphragmatic breathing or progressive muscle relaxation, dim lighting, and a brief cognitive-to-physiological cueing (e.g., warm bath). Schedule any worry to a designated ‘worry log’ only during a set window earlier in the evening.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Provides predictable physiological cues to reduce overall arousal while creating a cognitive boundary for intrusive replay and future-oriented worry; supports earlier sleep onset by aligning circadian cues with pre-sleep behavior.
solutions.measurement: Bedtime timestamp; objective sleep onset latency via diary; frequency/duration of nighttime replay (C01 episodes); nightly worry intensity (0–10); next-day mood/irritability (0–10).
solutions.safety_notes: No safety concerns; monitor for persistent insomnia (>4 weeks) or worsening mood; escalate to clinician if sleep remains severely impaired.
solutions.mapping_query: C01, C02, C03, C04, C05 (wind-down routine aimed at reducing nocturnal replay, worry, arousal, delayed sleep onset, and morning irritability)

solutions.predictor_variable: Cognitive Behavioral Therapy for Insomnia (CBT-I) tailored to nighttime rumination and anticipatory worry
solutions.solution_candidate: Deliver CBT-I components focusing on stimulus control, sleep restriction as indicated, cognitive restructuring, and a scheduled worry time. Include behavioral experiments to test beliefs about sleep safety and prepare tomorrow’s tasks earlier in the evening.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Addresses both content (rumination) and arousal (cognitive and physiological) while strengthening sleep drive and regularity, leading to improved sleep onset and reduced morning irritability.
solutions.measurement: Sleep diary metrics (latency, efficiency); frequency of rumination episodes (C01); daily worry log (C02); morning irritability scale (0–10); PSQI or sleep quality score.
solutions.safety_notes: Monitor stress response; adjust CBT-I pace if distress increases; escalate if suicidality emerges.
solutions.mapping_query: C01–C05 (CBT-I targets nocturnal replay, anticipatory worry, hyperarousal, delayed sleep onset, and morning irritability)

solutions.predictor_variable: Mindfulness-based sleep interventions (MBIs) to reduce cognitive hyperarousal
solutions.solution_candidate: Enroll in an 8-week MBIS or ACT-for-sleep program emphasizing nonjudgmental awareness of thoughts, body scans, and breath-based regulation to decenter intrusive content and calm arousal.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Fosters decentering from intrusive past-conversation replay and reduces subjective arousal, facilitating easier sleep onset and calmer mornings.
solutions.measurement: Mindfulness/of acceptance scales (e.g., FFMQ/MAAS); nightly arousal rating (0–10); sleep onset latency; morning mood.
solutions.safety_notes: No major safety concerns; ensure practice in a safe context; consider referral if dissociation or distress occurs.
solutions.mapping_query: C03, C02, C01 (MBIs reduce hyperarousal and intrusive thoughts)

solutions.predictor_variable: Evening thought diary with structured cognitive offloading
solutions.solution_candidate: Introduce a 5–10 minute ‘brain dump’ journaling at a fixed time before the wind-down period (and a brief plan for tomorrow). Use looks at content replay and worries, then reframe or postpone to a ‘worry window’.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Externalizes intrusive thoughts and reduces cognitive load before bed, decreasing likelihood of bedtime rumination and hyperarousal.
solutions.measurement: Frequency/duration of brain-dump entries; pre-sleep worry intensity; late-evening rumination episodes; next-day irritability.
solutions.safety_notes: Ensure journaling is non-triggering; avoid rumination without redirection to problem-solving; seek support if distress worsens.
solutions.mapping_query: C01, C02, C03 (thought diary targets replay, worry, and arousal)

solutions.predictor_variable: Morning light exposure and daylight optimization
solutions.solution_candidate: Engage in 20–30 minutes of bright light exposure soon after waking (outdoor time preferred; consider light therapy box if needed). Avoid bright light near bedtime.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Strengthens circadian alignment, reduces late-night wakefulness propensity, and improves morning mood/reactivity, thereby reducing sleep onset delays and irritability.
solutions.measurement: Wake time consistency; time in bright light; sleep onset latency; morning irritability ratings.
solutions.safety_notes: Bipolar spectrum caution with light exposure; monitor for effects on mood instability.
solutions.mapping_query: C04, C03, C05 (light improves circadian timing, arousal, and morning mood)

solutions.solution_candidate: Eliminate caffeine after 2 pm; avoid other stimulants in late day; monitor effects of withdrawal; adjust meals to avoid late heavy intake.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Reduces physiological arousal and sleep latency, contributing to earlier sleep onset and improved morning mood.
solutions.measurement: Caffeine intake log; sleep onset latency; next-day irritability.
solutions.safety_notes: Watch for caffeine withdrawal headaches or fatigue; ensure alternate strategies for alertness.
solutions.mapping_query: C03, C04, C05 (evening intake affects arousal, sleep onset, and morning mood)

solutions.solution_candidate: Upgrade sleep environment: blackout curtains, maintain 65–68F, white noise or soft sound, device-free 60 minutes before bed, remove distracting stimuli.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Reduces sensory arousal and environmental disruptions, facilitating calmer pre-sleep state and faster sleep onset.
solutions.measurement: Bedroom conditions log; sleep onset latency; subjective sleep quality; morning mood.
solutions.safety_notes: If room changes disrupt sleep ecology (noise complaints, temperature discomfort), adjust and re-check.
solutions.mapping_query: C03, C04 (environmental optimization lowers arousal and supports sleep onset)

solutions.predictor_variable: Interpersonal boundary and morning routine redesign to reduce irritability
solutions.solution_candidate: Institute a collaborative morning routine with a calm-start plan (limit negative talk, set expectations, and include 5 minutes of shared space for positive check-ins). Train brief communication boundaries.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Improved social context and reduced morning stress reactivity, potentially mitigating early-morning hostility and neurochemical arousal from negative social cues.
solutions.measurement: Morning mood and irritability ratings; perceived social support; frequency of negative morning interactions.
solutions.safety_notes: Ensure fairness in boundary setting; avoid conflict escalation; involve trusted support if necessary.
solutions.mapping_query: C05, C02, C03 (social boundary and routine affect irritability, worry, and arousal)

solutions.predictor_variable: Digital self-monitoring and feedback loop (sleep/mood diary with weekly review)
solutions.solution_candidate: Use a simple app or diary to log bedtimes, sleep onset latency, awakenings, daytime mood, and cognitive arousal; generate weekly trend reports and micro-goals.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Facilitates measurement-based care, enabling timely adjustments to targets and reinforcing progress; increases self-awareness of arousal patterns.
solutions.measurement: Daily sleep metrics; weekly trend reports; adherence to routines; mood/arousal indices.
solutions.safety_notes: Protect data privacy; ensure user consent; avoid over-monitoring which may increase anxiety.
solutions.mapping_query: C01–C05 (digital monitoring informs all target criteria)

solutions.predictor_variable: Regular daytime aerobic activity to modulate arousal and sleep pressure
solutions.solution_candidate: Engage in 30–45 minutes of moderate aerobic exercise most days, preferably earlier in the day; avoid intense workouts close to bedtime.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Enhances sleep homeostasis and reduces evening cognitive arousal, contributing to earlier sleep onset and improved next-day mood.
solutions.measurement: Exercise logs; sleep onset latency; morning irritability; perceived energy.
solutions.safety_notes: Medical clearance if new to exercise; monitor for injuries or excessive fatigue.
solutions.mapping_query: C03, C04 (physical activity reduces arousal and improves sleep timing)
